title: Survival watchdog auto-restart drops all Stage-2 pre-arming (no launch_cmd.json)
source: Code audit 2026-06-15 (launch/freeze scripts subagent), finding C1
why: Under the Jun-21 22:00 BST freeze the survival book runs autonomously for ~5 days. scripts/watchdog_monitor.py:53-56 restarts the survival service with a HARDCODED SURVIVAL_CMD lacking --lottery-arm, --lottery-risk-frac, --portfolio-kill, --flatten-at. If the process dies mid-week the watchdog resurrects it DISARMED and without deadlines — the opposite of 'pre-armed autonomous'. Worse: if the lottery sleeve had already entered, a disarmed restart fails (exit 2) and the watchdog enters a crash-loop. The OSR runner already solves this by persisting launch_cmd.json and replaying it verbatim (run_osr_live.py:201-205); the survival runner has no equivalent.
value_rationale: Breaks the core freeze premise: any mid-week crash silently disarms Stage 2 (lottery sleeve, equity-lock, flatten deadlines) or crash-loops. Directly affects autonomous survival + the Stage-2 variance bet. High.
scope: Survival runner (run_survival_live.py) persists its full argv to a launch_cmd.json on startup, mirroring the OSR runner pattern.
watchdog_monitor.py _do_restart() replays launch_cmd.json verbatim for the survival book (excluding --force) instead of the hardcoded SURVIVAL_CMD.
Test: restart reproduces all Stage-2 flags; crash-loop path eliminated.
recommendation: Copy the OSR launch_cmd.json mechanism. TDD: parity test that restart argv == launch argv minus --force.
out_of_scope: MT5 reconnection in run_loop (separate B-ID B0177).

history.note: From hackathon code audit, finding C1

history.reason: Implemented: run_survival_live persists launch_cmd.json (strips --force); watchdog _do_restart replays it, falls back to base cmd. 4 new tests, full suite 2202 passed.
